div.h3: Implementation Specification: Atomic Append & LSN Sequencing
div.p: Context: Multiple worker threads serialized via pthread_mutex_t. Invariant: Lock held only during memory-to-kernel write(). fsync() handled asynchronously per group-commit policy. Reference: Intel Software Developer’s Manual, Vol 3, Section 11.2.1

ol.li: Atomicity: The mutex ensures that write() syscalls do not interleave bytes at the kernel page-cache level.
LSN Monotonicity: LSN is assigned inside the lock to guarantee that disk order matches sequence number order.
Performance Optimization: We release the lock before calling fsync(). This allows multiple writers to fill the page cache while a previous batch is being flushed to platter/SSD.
